signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Fed Testimony
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5842 reflects a dominant G10 theme as NZD outperforms all peers while USD lags following softer PPI data and Fed Chair Warsh's testimony. Despite a 125bp carry deficit (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.5%), NZD strength is driven by its G10-leading 1W performance. Fed 3.75%. NZD COT LF net short -27,675 (18.0% long) creates a massive short-squeeze risk as the RBNZ maintains a hiking bias (real rate -0.58%) against a softening USD macro backdrop.
signals.watch_level: Watch for a break above 0.5900; next catalyst is the Fed meeting on 29 Jul.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI Divergence
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3498 tests session highs following weaker US PPI, but internal metrics signal exhaustion. While COT LF net long +17,228 (59.0% long) shows spec support, the negative CESI (net -7 misses) suggests UK data is consistently underperforming expectations. Options markets are pricing a significant 4.5pt Vol Risk Premium (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.5%) with a mild put skew (25d RR -0.62), indicating that institutional desks are paying up for downside protection despite the spot rally. This divergence suggests the move is driven by USD weakness rather than GBP strength.
signals.watch_level: Watch for failure at 1.3520; BoE meeting on 30 Jul is the primary risk.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.13 remains held above the 160.00 psychological threshold by a +262bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.60% vs TONA 0.98%). However, the options market is heavily skewed toward JPY calls (25d RR -1.39), signaling acute hedging against BoJ intervention or a carry unwind. A correlation break (z=-1.51σ) shows JPY is failing to attract safe-haven flows despite normal VIX levels (16.1), leaving the pair purely dependent on the yield gap. With JPY COT LF net short -104,231, any hawkish shift at the next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) risks a violent liquidation.
signals.watch_level: 160.00 remains the critical support; watch BoJ meeting on 31 Jul.

signals.title: AUD/CHF — Retail Divergence + Carry
signals.text: AUD/CHF at 0.5646 exhibits an extreme retail sentiment divergence with 90% of retail traders short. This contrarian signal aligns with a massive +435bp carry premium (RBA 4.35% vs SNB 0.0%) favoring the AUD. While AUD COT positioning is net long (+24,098), the retail crowd is aggressively fading the trend. In a 'normal volatility' regime (VIX 16.1), the carry trade remains the path of least resistance, suggesting retail capitulation risk if the pair extends higher.
signals.watch_level: Watch for retail short covering above 0.5700; RBA meeting on 11 Aug.
